message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Serological evaluation with SARS-CoV-2 specific IgG antibody will be an alternative way to know the pandemic of novel coronavirus disease (COVID-19) if the capacity for diagnostic PCR test is limited. The point-of-care test to detect SARS-CoV-2 specific IgG antibody in peripheral blood (
                  <jats:italic>n</jats:italic>
                  =202) was performed in two community clinics in Tokyo, Japan. The overall positive rate of SRAS-CoV-2 IgG antibody was 5.9% (95% confidence interval[CI]: 3.1-10.1). Higher rate was observed for healthcare workers (
                  <jats:italic>n</jats:italic>
                  =55, 9.1 [3.0-20.0]). The limitation on antibody tests includes low sensitivity and potent cross-reactivity with the previous coronavirus. Robust healthcare policy to efficiently monitor COVID-19 spread is warranted in Tokyo.
                </jats:p>
title: Preliminary Results of Seroprevalence of SARS-CoV-2 at Community Clinics in Tokyo

reference.unstructured: Guo L , Ren L , Yang S , et al. Profiling Early Humoral Response to Diagnose Novel Coronavirus Disease (COVID-19). Clin Infect Dis 2020.
